name: Tonbogiri
description: Tonbogiri, meaning "Dragonfly Cutter," is a legendary spear crafted by the renowned swordsmith Fujiwara Masazane. It is said that when the spear was tested, a dragonfly landed on its blade and was instantly cut in two, giving the weapon its formidable name. Tonbogiri became the signature weapon of Honda Tadakatsu, one of the Four Heavenly Kings of the Tokugawa, a fearless general who served Tokugawa Ieyasu faithfully throughout his life. The spear is not only a symbol of Tadakatsu's exceptional martial prowess but also a testament to his unwavering loyalty and courage. It is believed to possess supernatural sharpness and unparalleled balance, making it an extension of the wielder's will. Tonbogiri is more than just a weapon; it represents the ideals of Bushido, the warrior code, embodying honor, discipline, and unwavering dedication. Its legend continues to inspire awe and reverence, reminding us of the extraordinary feats of samurai warriors in Japanese history. The spear is a national treasure and a potent symbol of Japan's martial heritage, representing the pinnacle of craftsmanship and the embodiment of warrior spirit. It is often depicted in art, literature, and popular culture, cementing its place as one of the most iconic weapons in Japanese folklore. The spear is a reminder of the importance of skill, dedication, and unwavering loyalty in achieving greatness. Its legend serves as a moral compass, guiding individuals towards a path of honor and self-improvement.
subtitle: The Dragonfly Cutter: Honda Tadakatsu's Spear
origin: Crafted by Fujiwara Masazane for Honda Tadakatsu.
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly in a museum or private collection.

keyMyths.title: The Dragonfly Test
keyMyths.description: The spear earned its name when a dragonfly landed on its blade and was instantly cut in two, demonstrating its extraordinary sharpness. This event solidified the spear's reputation as a weapon of immense power.

keyMyths.title: Honda Tadakatsu's Invincibility
keyMyths.description: Honda Tadakatsu wielded Tonbogiri in countless battles and was never seriously wounded, leading many to believe the spear possessed protective qualities. His victories are attributed to his skill and the legendary spear.

sources.author: Stephen Turnbull
sources.work: Samurai: The World of the Warrior
sources.description: Provides historical context for samurai weaponry and tactics.
sources.text: Provides historical context for samurai weaponry and tactics.
